stimulus: Engineer Mary Anderson devised one of the world's most ubiquitous automotive features. In the early 1900s, she developed the windshield wiper, which ______blank of the manual cloth-and-snow-removal practices drivers had relied on for decades.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement and agreement between nouns. The singular verb "was" and the singular noun "replacement" both agree in number with the relative pronoun "which." In this context, "which" functions as a singular subject because it refers to the singular noun "the windshield wiper."

Choice B is incorrect because the plural verb "are" and the plural noun "replacements" don't agree in number with the singular noun phrase "the windshield wiper" that they're modifying. Choice C is incorrect because the plural verb "were" and the plural noun "replacements" don't agree in number with the singular noun phrase "the windshield wiper." Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn't agree in number with the singular noun phrase "the windshield wiper" that it's modifying.
